mnemonics: HIVE: Hadoop's SQL Voice (It's SQL-like for querying data stored in Hadoop)
keyPoints: HQL is used for querying and managing large datasets stored in Hadoop. It is not for transactional operations (OLTP) but for analytical processing (OLAP).
HQL syntax is very similar to SQL, making it easier for those familiar with relational databases to learn.
Hive translates HQL queries into MapReduce or Tez jobs that run on the Hadoop cluster, enabling distributed processing of big data.
commonMistakes: Using HQL for real-time, low-latency queries; it is designed for batch processing and is not fast like an RDBMS.
Forgetting that Hive uses schema-on-read, meaning the data's structure is applied when the data is queried, not when it is loaded.
